loungeAccess.conditions: Domestic: 3 visits/quarter, requires ₹60,000+ spend in the preceding calendar quarter (effective May 2026). International: 6 visits per calendar year via complimentary Priority Pass membership, no spend condition (so roughly 1-2 per quarter).

history.oldValue: 3 domestic lounge visits per quarter (12/year), no spend condition
history.newValue: 3 domestic lounge visits per quarter, requires ₹60,000+ spend in the preceding calendar quarter

source.name: CardExpert
source.document: HDFC Regalia Gold Now Requires Spends for Lounge Access, Introduces Boarding Edge

source.name: CardExpert
source.document: HDFC Regalia Gold Now Requires Spends for Lounge Access, Introduces Boarding Edge

faqTemplateAnswers.Is this card lifetime free?: No. It has a ₹2,500 + taxes joining and annual fee, waived on renewal if you spend ₹4,00,000 or more in the preceding year.
faqTemplateAnswers.Is it worth it?: It's still a solid mid-premium travel card thanks to reward point transfers to airline/hotel partners and the new Boarding Edge perks, but its lounge access is no longer unconditional — since May 2026 you must spend ₹60,000 in the prior quarter to unlock 3 domestic visits.
faqTemplateAnswers.Who should get this card?: Frequent domestic travellers who can consistently clear ₹60,000 in quarterly spend and want to transfer reward points to airline/hotel loyalty programs.
faqTemplateAnswers.Does it support UPI?: No. It is issued on the Visa network, and Visa credit cards cannot currently be linked to UPI apps in India.
faqTemplateAnswers.Can I get lounge access with this card?: Yes, but conditionally since May 2026: 3 domestic lounge visits per quarter require ₹60,000+ spend in the preceding quarter, while 6 international Priority Pass visits per year remain available with no spend condition.
faqTemplateAnswers.What income is required to qualify?: HDFC typically requires a net monthly income of about ₹1,00,000 (government employees) or ₹1,50,000 (private sector) for salaried applicants, or an ITR of ₹18 lakh+ for self-employed applicants.
faqTemplateAnswers.Who should avoid this card?: Occasional travellers who were relying on unconditional lounge access, or anyone whose quarterly card spend regularly falls short of ₹60,000.
faqTemplateAnswers.What are the best alternatives?: HDFC Infinia or HDFC Marriott Bonvoy for cardholders who want more dependable lounge access, or Axis Magnus/HDFC Diners Club Black for comparable travel-reward positioning.
